messages.content: Hey, getting back from a stand-up. I'm an engineer at Stripe — been here three years. Based in San Francisco. Coffee or it doesn't count.
Got it — engineer at Stripe in SF, coffee-fueled. What can I help with?
Also, had to take Biscuit on a long walk this morning before the meeting. He doesn't tolerate skipped walks.
Biscuit clearly runs a tight ship.
Big update — I just joined Notion as a product manager and moved to Berlin last month. Loving it so far.
Big shift! PM at Notion in Berlin. Congrats.
Walking Biscuit in the Tiergarten before the Notion offsite. Berlin in spring is something else.
Quick context: I'm vegetarian, and seriously allergic to shellfish — went to the ER once over it. Picking a restaurant for tomorrow.
Noted — vegetarian, shellfish allergy. I'll keep both in mind.
I love TypeScript, by the way. The type system has saved me from so many bugs at work this past year.
Hot take from today: TypeScript is fine for big projects but for one-off scripts I'd just reach for Python. The generics get annoying past a certain complexity.
Sorry, correction from earlier — when I said I went to the ER for the shellfish thing, it was actually just urgent care, not the ER. Bad memory.
